message:
refactor(storage): extract append_label_membership_filters helper

The list-issue and list-relations SQL builders repeated the same
label-AND / label-OR filter assembly inline at three call sites
(roughly 25 lines each). The shape was identical in every case:

  * for `labels_and = []`, no clause is appended;
  * for `labels_and = [single]`, append a single
    `AND issues.id IN (SELECT issue_id FROM labels WHERE label = ?)`
    so SQLite can use the labels(label) index for an indexed semi-join;
  * for `labels_and = [multi...]`, append one
    `AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM labels WHERE labels.issue_id = issues.id
    AND labels.label = ?)` per requested label so each label gets an
    independent existence check (correct semantics for AND-of-labels);
  * for `labels_or`, append a single
    `AND issues.id IN (SELECT issue_id FROM labels WHERE label IN (?,?,…))`
    with one placeholder per label.

This commit pulls that logic into a single
`append_label_membership_filters(sql, params, labels_and, labels_or)`
helper at the top of `src/storage/sqlite.rs` and replaces the three
inline expansions with calls into it. The helper takes the SQL string
and the SqliteValue parameter vector by mutable reference and pushes
both the clause text and the bound parameters in lockstep, so callers
no longer have to keep two parallel structures consistent by hand.

This is a pure refactor: the generated SQL and the bound parameter
order are byte-identical to the previous inline implementations for
every (labels_and, labels_or) combination, so existing query plans
and EXPLAIN outputs are unchanged.
